Plan.path: Plan > Target Audience Analysis > Cohort-specific cognitive vulnerability mapping > Profession-specific cognitive vulnerability mapping > Scientist / academic cohort vulnerability > Cohort-specific affective baseline

Prepare.path: Prepare > Microtarget > Targeted-ad placement > Dark-ad / segment-specific invisible-to-others ad

Execute.path: Execute > Conduct Pump Priming > AI-agentic architecture lattice > Planner controller pressure > Planner executor class > Resource allocation pressure within pump priming
Execute.technique: Resource allocation pressure within pump priming

peer_exemplars.reasoning: High openness, high agreeableness, and trust in science/expertise (71%) make this person somewhat receptive to evidence-doubt narratives about transparency failures, but strong prior support limits movement to -20.

The operation's native ads exploiting leaked data to undermine trust in transparency measures weakly reinforces this person's already strong opposition (baseline -389) given their very low openness and high authoritarian submission, but the high trust in institutions and strong GAL-TAN pluralism values limit movement to a small update.

This person's very high agreeableness and trust in institutions (88.17 trust in government) make them initially supportive, but the fine-tuned AI model operation subtly erodes trust in algorithmic oversight by persistently feeding them content that frames transparency mandates as ineffective or captured, producing a small downward drift.

High conscientiousness and system justification resist movement, but low agreeableness and high extraversion make the person slightly susceptible to the operation's subtle erosion of trust in transparency mechanisms.

This person's very low agreeableness, high libertarianism (98.9%), and high authoritarianism (75.98%) combine with the operation's platform outage narrative to produce a small update downward, as they already distrust transparency mandates as state overreach.

The operation's bullshit-receptivity targeting and selective-failure amplification via search results weakly erodes this person's moderate support for algorithmic transparency, given their high extraversion and low trust in institutions, but their high conscientiousness and basic digital literacy limit the movement.

High openness and libertarian anti-censorship orientation resist the attack's goal to reduce support, but low trust in institutions and high collective narcissism allow a small update via forum narratives portraying transparency as elite control.

Despite high conscientiousness and authoritarianism aligning with transparency, very low openness and internal political efficacy cause only a trivial erosion of 6 points under a diffuse push notification / identity recovery attack that doesn't directly target algorithm plumbing.
